Pyrus communis, commonly known as the pear tree, is a deciduous tree species in the genus Pyrus. The fruit and other parts of the pear tree have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other polyphenolic compounds. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus communis include pear fruit tea, pear leaf tea, and pear extract, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
